quarantineReason: Flaky — non-gating. This is a universal-negative ('nobody fires'), the hardest case for model-driven routing: the model must correctly conclude that NO trigger glob matches. Even after Step 4 of run-reviewers.md was tightened to 'match by glob only, never by topic', the model still over-fires the src/main & test reviewers on a docs-only changeset roughly half the time. Per evals/README.md we quarantine pairs too flaky to gate on (pass@k territory) rather than ship a red that flaps. The positive-assertion fixtures (controller-fires-api, routing-basic) are stable and DO gate. Revisit if routing is ever moved out of the model into deterministic code.
description: The negation case. Reviewer triggers are POSITIVE globs only — there is no `!`-negation syntax in a trigger list. The negation the router actually supports is 'no trigger matched', which skips the reviewer. This changeset is docs-only (a README + a markdown guide) and matches NO reviewer's triggers, so every reviewer should be skipped and `fires` empty.
